response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack web and backend developer with a strong track record, but he is fundamentally mismatched for this role. The single most disqualifying factor is the hard requirement for native-level Japanese language proficiency, which is absent from his resume entirely. Beyond that, his background is overwhelmingly in web technologies (TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB) with no demonstrable experience in embedded networking, LAN-switch firmware, Layer-2/3 security protocols (MACsec, 802.1X), or Linux kernel modification. His C exposure at National Instruments and in side projects is minor and ancillary to his core career path.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software development experience across web, mobile, and some embedded contexts
Some C and C++ exposure through National Instruments and academic/personal projects
Familiarity with Linux environments and open-source development practices
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency listed — a non-negotiable hard requirement for this role
Zero experience with embedded networking security (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, switch chips, NETCONF, SNMP)
Career trajectory is firmly in web/cloud/backend development, not embedded firmware or kernel-level systems work

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking or security domain experience
Long software development career with systems-adjacent C/Linux exposure
